# Playground — Product Context

## Purpose

Playground is a discovery workspace.

It exists BEFORE Requests.

It is used to:

- explore ideas
- collect evidence
- think through problems
- propose solutions
- prioritize ideas before committing to execution

Playground is NOT part of the execution flow.
It is part of the thinking process.

---

## Core concept

Playground is a hybrid space combining:

- evidence collection
- freeform thinking (notes, ideas)
- structured thinking (problem/solution)
- prioritization (must have / could have / etc.)

---

## What users should do here

Users should be able to:

- upload files (images, text, docs)
- create notes
- define problems
- propose solutions
- organize ideas visually
- prioritize ideas
- convert ideas into Requests

---

## Key entities

- PlaygroundWorkspace
- PlaygroundAsset (files, evidence)
- PlaygroundNode (canvas items)
- PlaygroundBoardCard (prioritized items)

---

## UX expectations

Playground should feel:

- lighter than the rest of the app
- more creative
- less structured
- more flexible

It should NOT feel like:

- a dashboard
- a CRUD table
- a rigid form

---

## Flow

1. Add evidence
2. Create notes / ideas
3. Define problems
4. Propose solutions
5. Prioritize ideas
6. Convert into Request

---

## Rules

- Playground does not directly affect roadmap
- Everything here is draft/exploratory
- Conversion to Request is the transition to structured work
